impact:
V1E THD rises with frequency on the pedal but FALLS on the plugin — the slope has the wrong sign:
            100Hz  200Hz  400Hz
  pedal      4.5    10.5   22.3   <- RISES
  plugin     5.9     3.6    0.9   <- FALLS
NEW (2026-07-16, comprehensive_data.json, full-band Farina): the pedal keeps climbing well past the previously-anchored 400Hz — ~31% @1016Hz and ~35% @1280Hz (D0.50, drv-12) vs the plugin's ~1.5%/3.4%. The gap is far larger and wider than the audit recorded. THD is a RATIO so this data is immune to the capture level-normalization and needed no gain-match fix.

CANDIDATE 1 IS NOW RULED OUT BY DATA. The audit's first candidate was "the twin-T notch is upstream of the clip — if the real notch is shallower/narrower than modelled, the pedal would keep driving the clip". cascade_analysis.py shows the twin-T notch region (600-1000Hz) matches within ~1.2dB at LOW drive on V1E (D0.50 -1.11dB, D0.60 -1.19dB) and only diverges at high drive (D1.00 -11.41dB). A filter error would be present at EVERY drive. The notch filter is therefore CORRECT; the drive-dependence is the fault. Do not re-investigate the notch component values.

This makes the remaining cause drive/clip-stage behaviour, SHARED with ISS-002 (gap B) and ISS-004 (gap D): V2 shows the same drive-dependent twin-T divergence (D0.25 +4.27 -> D0.90 -2.75, swing 7.03dB). Solve once, not three times. Note the sign is opposite between revisions, so it is not a single shared constant.

Reproduce: python3.11 analysis/gap_audit.py --mode thd --rev V1E ; python3.11 analysis/cascade_analysis.py (section B).

*** NEW EVIDENCE FOLDED IN FROM ISS-013 (2026-07-16, ISS-013 closed as INVALID) ***
The LF band is ANOTHER FACE OF THIS SAME ISSUE — and it is a cleaner, more sensitive probe of the
clip-onset gap than THD is. Do not open a separate LF issue; one was opened (ISS-013) and closed.

MEASURED (analysis/iss013_drive_lf.py — plugin only, FIXED 200 Hz reference, no peak-finding,
sweeping DRIVE 0 -> 1.00): the plugin's LF is essentially DRIVE-INDEPENDENT — swing <= 2.24 dB at
40-100 Hz on ALL THREE revisions. But cascade_analysis.py section B's LF column (plugin MINUS capture)
swings 9.10 dB on V1E and 3.92 dB on V2 across drive. Since the plugin barely moves, that swing is
almost entirely THE PEDAL'S OWN drive-dependence: the pedal's LF compresses with drive and the
plugin's does not.

WHY LF IS THE MOST SENSITIVE BAND FOR THIS: the twin-T scoops ~800 Hz, so LF passes at the FULL drive
gain and is the LOUDEST thing in the wet path — it therefore hits the pedal's clip FIRST and hardest.
A plugin that under-clips holds LF flat while the pedal compresses it. This is exactly the standing
CLAUDE.md note that on the -30 dBFS clean sweep at D1.00 "the plugin barely clips ... while the pedal
already compresses", and it is the same fault as this issue's THD-slope symptom, seen in the FR
instead of in the harmonics.

WHY THIS IS USEFUL TO THE FIX: the LF/FR view is immune to the THD measurement traps recorded in
CLAUDE.md (V1E THD anchors are 100/200 Hz ONLY, because 400 Hz sits on the ~430 Hz bridged-T and
800 Hz on the twin-T notch — both notch the FUNDAMENTAL and inflate THD). So use the fixed-frequency
LF-vs-drive table as a SECOND, independent target when fitting clip onset: a correct fix must make the
plugin's LF compress with drive the way the pedal's does, AND fix the THD slope. Two independent
symptoms, one mechanism — which is the strongest available evidence that clip onset is genuinely the
shared root cause across ISS-001/002/004.

CAVEAT (N-004): anchor any LF reading at 40-100 Hz. 25 Hz is estimator noise in this harness (V1L's
25 Hz reading swings 21 dB NON-MONOTONICALLY across drive), and it has already produced two wrong
conclusions.
title: V1E THD-vs-frequency slope has wrong sign
